name: GTE Large
summary: GTE Large is Alibaba's General Text Embeddings large model, producing 1024-dimensional dense vectors trained on a large-scale corpus of relevance pairs. It achieves strong performance on the MTEB benchmark and is fully open-weight, allowing self-hosted deployment without API dependencies.
use_when: Self-hosted semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation pipelines where data privacy prohibits API usage. Fine-tuning on domain-specific corpora using contrastive learning on labeled relevance pairs. Multi-tenant vector search systems where embedding latency must be predictable and controllable.
avoid_when: Applications requiring cutting-edge MTEB performance where OpenAI's text-embedding-3-large or Cohere models hold the top positions. Environments where GPU inference is unavailable and CPU embedding of long documents becomes a bottleneck. Use cases requiring 3K+ dimensional embeddings for maximum representational capacity.
pros: Fully open weights enabling self-hosted deployment, fine-tuning, and complete control over inference infrastructure and data privacy
Strong MTEB performance on retrieval and classification tasks, often competitive with API-only models at a fraction of the operational cost
1024-dimensional output strikes a balance between representational quality and vector database storage efficiency
Native SentenceTransformers compatibility simplifies integration with existing RAG pipelines, ChromaDB, and FAISS indexing workflows
cons: MTEB scores slightly trail the top API-only models on certain clustering and pair classification tasks where larger models excel
1024 dimensions may limit representational capacity for highly heterogeneous corpora compared to 3072-dimensional alternatives
Requires GPU for batch inference on large datasets; CPU embedding of long documents is significantly slower than API-based solutions
Fine-tuning requires labeled relevance pairs and contrastive learning expertise, raising the barrier to domain adaptation compared to plug-and-play APIs

key_hyperparams.name: batch_size
key_hyperparams.note: Number of sentences encoded simultaneously. Increase to 64-128 on high-memory GPUs for higher throughput. Decrease to 8-16 on memory-constrained devices.

key_hyperparams.name: normalize_embeddings
key_hyperparams.note: Whether to L2-normalize output embeddings. Set to 'true' for cosine similarity search and dot-product retrieval. Set to 'false' only when using learned distance metrics.

quick_start:
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('thenlper/gte-large')

sentences = [
    'This is an example sentence',
    'Each sentence is converted to an embedding'
]
embeddings = model.encode(sentences, normalize_embeddings=True, batch_size=32)
print(embeddings.shape)  # (2, 1024)
decision_notes: Choose GTE Large for self-hosted embedding with strong MTEB performance. Use OpenAI text-embedding-3-large for top-tier accuracy when API dependency is acceptable.
